report_text_preview: EXAMINATION:  CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST COMPARISON:  None CLINICAL HISTORY:  Neuro deficit  acute  stroke suspected COMMENTS:  Axial noncontrast CT scan slices of the head were obtained. Sagittal and coronal reconstructions were obtained. CT imaging was performed with iterative reconstruction technique and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose. FINDINGS:  There is mild mucosal thickening in the visualized sinuses. There is calcific atherosclerotic change in the arteries at the skull base. There is a small focus of low density that is cortical and subcortical at the left occipital lobe. No acute  hemorrhage. There is mild to moderate decreased density in the white matter. Small focus calcification is noted in the left cerebellum. IMPRESSION:  Left occipital acute stroke suspected. Correlation with MRI is recommended.
